title: Sick of missing the dip. Which AI tool is actually worth the money?
author: Aware_Living_1595
selftext:
Hey guys, I’ve been trying to find a solid way to scan for stocks that have dropped below their average price without spending all day staring at charts. I’ve been looking at TrendSpider, Trade Ideas, Danelfin, Tickeron, and AInvest.

Problem is, they all claim to be "the best" and the subscriptions aren't cheap. Has anyone here actually used these for more than a week?

I'm specifically looking for something that's good at catching bounces/dips. I don't want to buy a "falling knife" that just keeps tanking. Is the Holly AI in Trade Ideas actually legit for this? Or is TrendSpider better if I’m more into technicals?

Honest opinions, trying to figure out if these tools actually help you make money or if I'm better off just sticking to a free screener and saving my cash. Thanks!

comments.author: Automatic-Essay2175
comments.body:
If you’re going to spend money on this shit just buy a market data subscription and program your screener. 

You can even use AI for that.

comments.author: SergeiStorm
comments.body:
None of these tools will find an edge for you - they just automate screening faster.
Figure out your entry rules manually first, then consider paying to scale them.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

comments.author: Intelligent-Mess71
comments.body:
Most of these tools are just automating a rule you could define yourself, they’re not magically “catching the dip,” they’re scanning conditions like price vs moving average or momentum shifts.

For example, a simple dip idea is price pulling back to the 20 or 50 MA while the higher timeframe trend is still up, any decent screener or AI tool is just flagging that setup faster, not deciding if it’s a good trade for you.

The reality check is people lose money with these too, because they still take every signal without context, or ignore risk rules, same way people “breach” prop evals by overtrading even with good setups. The tool doesn’t stop you from taking a bad entry or sizing wrong.

If you’re going the prop route later, this matters even more, since it’s all evaluation plus funded account path in a simulated environment, and rules like daily loss or max drawdown don’t care if the signal came from AI or a basic screener.

Personally I’d test the logic on a free screener first, then see if paying for speed or automation actually improves your execution, not just your expectations.

Are you mainly trading stocks intraday or swing?

comments.body:
